prompt: A 1925 Soviet Constructivist poster by Alexander Rodchenko. No text, no labels. The composition represents a Transformer Architecture. Two tall, vertical towers of stacked black and white geometric rectangles (Encoder and Decoder) are connected by radiating, intersecting sharp red diagonal beams (Self-Attention). At the base, rhythmic repeating gear-notches provide a foundation (Positional Encoding). The layout is an aggressive diagonal composition with high contrast. Colors: flat signal red, deep black, and a background of aged, porous tan paper. Visible lithographic ink bleed and grainy paper texture. No gradients, no photography, no 3D shading.
tradition_approach: Rodchenko-inspired diagonal composition using photomontage-style flat geometry, a restricted red-black-cream palette, and rhythmic mechanical repetition to mirror parallel processing blocks.
